title: teucrium gnaphalodes subsp. ilerdense
intro: Teucrium gnaphalodes subsp. ilerdense, also known as Ilerdian germander, is a subspecies of perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains carnosic acid, responsible for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Ilerdian germander include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Ilerdian germander can be prepared through various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and skin problems.
